enrichment.short_description: AI legal copilot that reviews, drafts, and compares contracts directly inside Microsoft Word.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spellbook is an AI contract review and drafting copilot for Microsoft Word, used by 4,500+ legal teams in 80+ countries.

features.title: Review
features.description: Redlines contracts and flags risks directly inside Microsoft Word.

features.title: Draft
features.description: Creates clauses and full documents from scratch or from an organization's precedent library.

features.title: Compare
features.description: Benchmarks a contract's terms against thousands of similar agreements.

features.title: Ask
features.description: Answers legal questions with cited sources pulled from relevant documents.

features.title: Playbooks
features.description: Encodes an organization's negotiation standards so reviews stay consistent.

features.title: Associate
features.description: An AI agent that carries out multi-document legal workflows.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects to email, Slack, and Salesforce alongside its Word add-in.

pros: Works directly inside Microsoft Word, requiring no workflow change for lawyers
Used by 4,500+ legal teams across 80+ countries
SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA, and PIPEDA compliant with Zero Data Retention options
Covers the full contract lifecycle: review, drafting, comparison, and Q&A
cons: Pricing is not published and requires booking a demo for a custom quote
Primarily built around a Microsoft Word workflow
Per-seat licensing means costs scale with team size

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Spellbook?
faqs.answer: Yes, Spellbook offers a 7-day free trial for lawyers and legal teams.

faqs.question: How is Spellbook priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is determined by the number of team members on a license; a custom quote is provided after a demo.

faqs.question: Where does Spellbook run?
faqs.answer: Spellbook runs as an add-in inside Microsoft Word.

faqs.question: What AI models power Spellbook?
faqs.answer: Spellbook says it is powered by advanced large language models, including GPT-5 and Opus, tuned for commercial legal work.

use_cases.title: Contract redlining at scale
use_cases.description: In-house legal teams use Spellbook to review and flag risks in incoming contracts inside Word.

use_cases.title: Drafting from precedent
use_cases.description: Law firms draft new clauses and documents using their own precedent libraries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spellbook?
paragraphs: Spellbook is an AI-powered legal copilot that works as an add-in inside Microsoft Word, helping lawyers review, draft, and compare contracts without leaving their existing document workflow.

The platform offers Review for redlining and risk-flagging, Draft for generating clauses and documents, Compare for benchmarking terms against similar agreements, Ask for cited legal Q&A, and Associate, an AI agent for multi-document workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spellbook is for
paragraphs: Spellbook is aimed at in-house legal teams and law firms that want to speed up contract review and drafting. The company reports it is used by 4,500+ legal teams in 80+ countries, including teams at Dropbox, Atkins Realis, Asics, and Kennedys Law.

enrichment.short_description: AI platform for law firms and in-house legal teams covering research, drafting, and document review.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Harvey is an AI platform for legal and professional services, used by 60+ AmLaw 100 firms for research, drafting, and review.

features.title: Assistant
features.description: Domain-specific AI for document analysis, legal research, and drafting.

features.title: Vault
features.description: Secure document storage, organization, and bulk analysis.

features.title: Knowledge
features.description: Research across legal, regulatory, and tax domains.

features.title: Agents
features.description: Autonomous tools that execute complex legal workflows end-to-end.

features.title: Contract Intelligence
features.description: Extracts insights and accelerates contract review.

features.title: Command Center
features.description: Analytics and organizational AI usage insights.

features.title: Shared Spaces
features.description: Secure collaboration on matters across organizations.

features.title: Harvey Mobile
features.description: Mobile access to continue legal workflows away from a desktop.

pros: Used by 60+ AmLaw 100 firms and named enterprise customers such as Vinson & Elkins, Reed Smith, and A&O Shearman
Certified to SOC2 II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 42001, GDPR, and CCPA standards
Available in US, EU, and Australia regions with SAML SSO and enterprise access controls
Customers report averaging 20+ hours saved per professional per month
cons: Pricing is not published; a demo request is required to get a quote
Positioning and named customers skew toward large law firms and enterprise legal teams
No public free trial or self-serve signup

faqs.question: How much does Harvey cost?
faqs.answer: Harvey does not publish pricing; prospective customers must request a demo to get a quote.

faqs.question: Who uses Harvey?
faqs.answer: Harvey is used by 60+ AmLaw 100 firms and legal teams, including Vinson & Elkins, Reed Smith, Deutsche Telekom, and A&O Shearman.

faqs.question: What security certifications does Harvey have?
faqs.answer: Harvey holds SOC2 II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, and ISO 42001 certifications, and supports GDPR and CCPA compliance.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile version of Harvey?
faqs.answer: Yes, Harvey Mobile provides mobile access to continue legal workflows.

use_cases.title: Large-scale document review
use_cases.description: AmLaw 100 firms use Harvey's Vault to organize and bulk-analyze large sets of documents.

use_cases.title: Multi-jurisdiction legal research
use_cases.description: Legal teams use Harvey's Knowledge feature to research across legal, regulatory, and tax domains.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Harvey?
paragraphs: Harvey is an AI software platform built for legal and professional services, offering tools for document analysis, legal research, drafting, contract intelligence, and autonomous agent-driven workflows.

The platform includes Assistant, Vault, Knowledge, Agents, Contract Intelligence, Command Center, Shared Spaces, and a mobile app, and is certified against SOC2 II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, and ISO 42001 standards.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Harvey is for
paragraphs: Harvey targets law firms, including AmLaw 100 firms, in-house legal departments, and enterprise legal teams that need to automate research, drafting, and document review at scale.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based legal case management platform with firm-wide AI agents, built on the LOIS system.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Filevine is a legal case management platform with firm-wide AI agents (LOIS), serving 6,000+ clients and 100,000+ users.

features.title: Firm-wide AI agents
features.description: AI agents that operate across multiple matters simultaneously, not just single tasks.

features.title: Structured legal graph
features.description: Built on 40M+ cases to support comprehensive case analysis.

features.title: Read/write AI actions
features.description: AI can generate documents, assign tasks, and manage deadlines directly in the system.

features.title: Document management
features.description: Centralized storage and organization of case documents.

features.title: Contract lifecycle management
features.description: Tools for managing contracts from drafting through execution.

features.title: Depositions
features.description: Scheduling, hosting, and analysis tools for depositions.

features.title: Vinesign
features.description: Built-in eSignature capability.

features.title: Medical record chronology
features.description: Tools to organize and summarize medical records for case work.

pros: Free trial available before committing to a subscription
Firm-wide AI agents work across multiple matters, not just individual tasks
Structured legal graph built on 40M+ cases
Serves 6,000+ clients and 100,000+ users across firm sizes and practice areas
cons: Pricing is not published; packages are custom-quoted after a sales conversation
Broad, firm-wide feature set may be more than very small solo practices need
Specific plan tiers and limits are not disclosed publicly

faqs.question: Does Filevine offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Filevine offers a free trial so firms can evaluate the platform before subscribing.

faqs.question: How is Filevine priced?
faqs.answer: Filevine does not publish prices; all packages are custom-built and quoted after a demo.

faqs.question: What is LOIS?
faqs.answer: LOIS (Legal Operating Intelligence System) is Filevine's AI platform layer that powers firm-wide agents, drafting, and case analysis.

faqs.question: What practice areas does Filevine support?
faqs.answer: Filevine is used across practice areas including personal injury, family law, mass torts, immigration, insurance defense, criminal defense, and estate planning, as well as by government legal offices.

use_cases.title: Firm-wide case operations
use_cases.description: Law firms use Filevine's AI agents to manage tasks and deadlines across many matters at once.

use_cases.title: Personal injury case tracking
use_cases.description: Personal injury practices use Filevine to track medical records, deadlines, and settlement progress.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Filevine?
paragraphs: Filevine is a legal case management and AI platform built around LOIS (Legal Operating Intelligence System), designed to operate across an entire firm's matters rather than one task at a time.

It combines document management, contract lifecycle management, depositions, eSignatures (Vinesign), and analytics, with AI agents that can read and write into the system to generate documents and manage deadlines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Filevine is for
paragraphs: Filevine serves law firms of all sizes, from solo practitioners to Big Law, across practice areas such as personal injury, family law, mass torts, immigration, insurance defense, and criminal defense, as well as government legal offices and in-house counsel.

enrichment.short_description: Salesforce-built legal operations platform combining case management, billing, and AI-driven workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Litify is a Salesforce-based legal operations platform for law firms and corporate legal departments, founded in 2016.

features.title: Intake Management
features.description: Dynamic questionnaires and client portals for qualifying new cases.

features.title: Matter Management
features.description: Centralized case handling with built-in automation.

features.title: Document Management
features.description: AI-powered document generation and PDF editing.

features.title: Time & Billing
features.description: Automated time capture and billing optimization.

features.title: Spend Management
features.description: Legal expense tracking, budgeting, and invoice management.

features.title: Reporting & Analytics
features.description: No-code reporting engine with customizable dashboards.

features.title: Litify AI
features.description: Agentic AI workflows embedded across the platform.

pros: Built on the Salesforce platform, giving firms familiar CRM-style infrastructure
No-code reporting engine for customizable dashboards without IT support
Founded in 2016 with 170+ employees across two US offices
Embedded AI (Litify AI) spans intake, documents, and reporting
cons: Pricing is not published and requires a demo request
Built on Salesforce, which may add platform overhead for firms unfamiliar with it
No public free trial mentioned

faqs.question: When was Litify founded?
faqs.answer: Litify was founded in 2016.

faqs.question: Where is Litify headquartered?
faqs.answer: Litify has offices in New York, NY and New Orleans, LA.

faqs.question: What platform is Litify built on?
faqs.answer: Litify is built on the Salesforce platform.

faqs.question: How much does Litify cost?
faqs.answer: Litify does not publish pricing; interested firms must request a demo for a quote.

use_cases.title: Personal injury case management
use_cases.description: Firms handling personal injury and insurance defense cases use Litify's matter management and automation.
use_cases.audience: Personal injury and insurance defense firms

use_cases.title: Corporate legal operations
use_cases.description: In-house legal departments use Litify's spend management and analytics to track legal costs and matters.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Litify?
paragraphs: Litify is a cloud-based legal operations platform built on Salesforce, combining intake management, matter management, document management, time and billing, spend management, and reporting into one system.
Founded in 2016, the company describes Litify as a 'platform of action' that automates workflows and embeds agentic AI (Litify AI) across intake, document generation, and analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Litify is for
paragraphs: Litify is aimed at law firms handling personal injury, insurance defense, and immigration cases, as well as corporate legal departments and enterprise legal teams that need advanced analytics and compliance controls.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud practice management software combining matter management with built-in trust and business accounting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CosmoLex is cloud practice management software with built-in trust accounting, offering a free 10-day trial.

features.title: Trust & business accounting
features.description: Built-in three-way reconciliation for trust and business accounts.

features.title: Time and billable tracking
features.description: Automated time capture and billable hour tracking.

features.title: Matter-centric case management
features.description: Organizes documents, tasks, and communications around each matter.

features.title: Client intake automation
features.description: Automated lead tracking and intake workflows.

features.title: Invoicing and payments
features.description: Invoice generation with integrated payment processing.

features.title: Document management
features.description: Secure document storage and client communication.

features.title: Financial reporting
features.description: 100+ real-time financial reporting options with full audit trails.

pros: Free 10-day trial with no credit card required
Built-in trust accounting with three-way reconciliation, reducing the need for separate accounting software
100+ real-time financial reports with full audit trails for compliance
Integrates with QuickBooks Online, Outlook, Google Workspace, Box, Dropbox, and NetDocuments
cons: Pricing is not published on the website; requires contacting sales or scheduling a demo
Website builder is only bundled in the Elite + Website tier
All named plans (Standard, Elite, Elite + Website) show identical core features, so differences are limited to add-ons

faqs.question: Does CosmoLex offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, CosmoLex offers a free 10-day trial with the same functionality as a paid subscription, aside from a single bank feed limitation.

faqs.question: Where is CosmoLex based?
faqs.answer: CosmoLex is based in West Palm Beach, Florida, and is part of ProfitSolv.

faqs.question: Does CosmoLex integrate with QuickBooks?
faqs.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online integration is available on the Standard and Elite tiers.

faqs.question: What plans does CosmoLex offer?
faqs.answer: CosmoLex offers Standard, Elite, and Elite + Website plans; pricing for each requires contacting sales.

use_cases.title: Trust accounting compliance
use_cases.description: Solo and small firms use CosmoLex's built-in trust accounting to stay compliant without separate accounting software.
use_cases.audience: Solo practitioners and small law firms

use_cases.title: Consolidating practice tools
use_cases.description: Mid-sized firms use CosmoLex to replace multiple disconnected billing and case management tools with one system.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CosmoLex?
paragraphs: CosmoLex is a cloud-based practice management platform that combines matter management, trust and business accounting, billing, time tracking, and document management in one system.

It is part of ProfitSolv and is based in West Palm Beach, Florida. Distinguishing features across its Standard, Elite, and Elite + Website plans include matter status boards, workflow automation, and an optional bundled website builder.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who CosmoLex is for
paragraphs: CosmoLex is built for solo practitioners, small firms, and mid-sized law practices that want to consolidate case management, billing, and trust accounting into a single tool instead of using multiple disconnected systems.

enrichment.short_description: Time tracking and billing software for law firms and hourly professionals, with iOS and Android apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TimeSolv is time and billing software with mobile apps, trust accounting, and a 10-day free trial for law firms.

features.title: Automatic time tracking
features.description: Mobile app timer with offline access and matter shortcuts.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Customizable invoice templates with auto-populated billing data.

features.title: TimeSolvPay
features.description: Built-in payment processing accepting credit cards and ACH transfers.

features.title: Trust accounting
features.description: Three-way reconciliation and trust reporting for compliance.

features.title: Project management
features.description: Task assignment, timeline monitoring, and budget control.

features.title: Client portal
features.description: Secure access to documents and invoices for clients.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: 30+ customizable performance and financial reports.

pros: 10-day free trial with no credit card required
Native iOS and Android mobile apps with offline time entry
Integrates with QuickBooks, NetDocuments, Dropbox, LawPay, AccountEdge, OneDrive, Outlook, and Xero
Built-in trust accounting with three-way reconciliation
cons: Pricing for the Pro and Legal plans is not published; based on plan tier and add-ons
Add-on modules (CRM, website, payments) are priced and billed separately from the core plans
Full feature set (trust accounting, LEDES billing) is limited to the Legal plan

faqs.question: Does TimeSolv offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, TimeSolv offers a 10-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does TimeSolv have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, TimeSolv has native iOS and Android apps with a built-in timer and offline access.

faqs.question: What is the difference between the Pro and Legal plans?
faqs.answer: The Legal plan adds conflict checking, matter budgets, LEDES billing, split billing, and trust accounting on top of the Pro plan's features.

faqs.question: What integrations does TimeSolv support?
faqs.answer: TimeSolv integrates with QuickBooks, NetDocuments, Dropbox, LawPay, AccountEdge, OneDrive, Outlook, and Xero.

use_cases.title: Billable hour tracking on the go
use_cases.description: Professionals use TimeSolv's mobile app to track billable time with or without an internet connection.

use_cases.title: Trust accounting compliance
use_cases.description: Law firms use TimeSolv's Legal plan for compliant three-way trust reconciliation and reporting.

features: Integrations with Outlook, O365, LawPay, Xero, QuickBooks

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TimeSolv?
paragraphs: TimeSolv is time tracking and billing software that helps professionals capture billable hours, generate invoices, process payments, and manage projects.

It offers Pro and Legal plans, native iOS and Android mobile apps, built-in TimeSolvPay payment processing, and add-ons for CRM and website building. TimeSolv is part of ProfitSolv, based in West Palm Beach, Florida.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TimeSolv is for
paragraphs: TimeSolv is built for law firms, consultants, accountants, freelancers, PR firms, and other hourly, service-based businesses that need to track time and bill clients.

enrichment.short_description: Legal time and billing software with per-user tiered pricing, trust accounting, and mobile apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bill4Time is legal time and billing software starting at $39/user/month, with a 14-day free trial and mobile apps.

features.title: Time tracking
features.description: Desktop and mobile timers with calendar-based and manual time entry.

features.title: Billing & invoicing
features.description: Customizable templates and batch, matter-based invoicing.

features.title: Case management
features.description: Centralized file storage, task assignment, and client collaboration.

features.title: Online payments
features.description: Native processing for credit cards, eCheck, and ACH with payment links.

features.title: Trust accounting
features.description: Compliance tools for IOLTA and ABA requirements (Legal plans).

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Interactive dashboards for collections, aging invoices, and firm performance.

features.title: Client portal
features.description: Secure document sharing and transparent billing access for clients.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Published, transparent per-user pricing across four tiers
Legal-specific tiers add trust accounting, conflict checking, and LEDES export
Available on web, desktop, iOS, and Android
cons: No permanent free plan
Trust accounting and conflict checking require the Legal Pro or Legal Enterprise tier
Advanced reports and dedicated account management are reserved for Enterprise tiers

faqs.question: How much does Bill4Time cost?
faqs.answer: Bill4Time starts at $39/user/month for Time & Billing when billed annually ($49/user/month billed monthly); Legal Pro and Enterprise tiers cost more.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bill4Time offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Bill4Time support trust accounting?
faqs.answer: Yes, trust accounting is included in the Legal Pro and Legal Enterprise plans.

faqs.question: Does Bill4Time have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bill4Time offers iOS and Android mobile apps.

use_cases.title: Solo and small firm billing
use_cases.description: Solo practitioners and small firms use Bill4Time's Time & Billing plan for time tracking and invoicing.
use_cases.audience: Solo practitioners and small law firms

use_cases.title: Trust-compliant legal billing
use_cases.description: Law firms use the Legal Pro or Legal Enterprise plans for IOLTA-compliant trust accounting and LEDES billing.
use_cases.audience: Law firms with trust accounting needs

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bill4Time?
paragraphs: Bill4Time is legal time and billing software that helps firms track billable hours, manage cases, invoice clients, and process payments in one platform.
It offers four published pricing tiers, from a general Time & Billing plan up to Legal Enterprise with trust accounting and custom LEDES export, plus web, desktop, iOS, and Android access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bill4Time is for
paragraphs: Bill4Time serves law firms of all sizes, from solo practitioners to larger firms, as well as other hourly professionals such as accountants, architects, consultants, contractors, and healthcare providers.

name: Centerbase
skip_reason: Official website (centerbase.com) could not be fetched: repeated SSL certificate verification failures across https/http and www/non-www variants and multiple subpages (homepage, features, about). No other officially-sourced page was reachable to verify facts, so per policy this entry is skipped rather than relying on unverified third-party aggregator estimates.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Actionstep is cloud practice management software for midsize law firms, used by 5,000+ firms with 50+ integrations.

features.description: Client-facing portal for communication and a firm knowledge center.

features.title: Billing & trust accounting

features.title: CRM & client intake

features.description: AI features for time capture, client intake assistance, and administrative tasks.

50+ integrations including Microsoft 365, Xero, Zapier, and DocuSign
AI features for time capture and client intake automation
Native iOS and Android mobile apps
cons: Pricing is not published; priced per user plus implementation fees, quote required

Pricing and availability vary by region (US, UK, AU, NZ, Canada)

faqs.question: How is Actionstep priced?
faqs.answer: Actionstep is priced per user plus implementation fees, with a custom quote required; it does not publish specific rates.

faqs.question: Where is Actionstep headquartered?
faqs.answer: Actionstep's office is located at 1743 Wazee St #200, Denver, CO 80202, and the company has 5 global offices.

faqs.question: Does Actionstep have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Actionstep offers iOS and Android mobile apps.

faqs.question: What integrations does Actionstep support?
faqs.answer: Actionstep offers 50+ integrations, including Microsoft Office 365, Xero, Zapier, DocuSign, and InfoTrack.

use_cases.title: Scaling a midsize firm
use_cases.description: Midsize law firms use Actionstep's workflow automation and CRM to manage growth without adding administrative headcount.

use_cases.title: Multi-practice-area management
use_cases.description: Firms spanning bankruptcy, family, real estate, and litigation practices use Actionstep's matter management across all case types.

pricing_plans.price: Priced per user plus implementation fees
Priced per user plus implementation fees

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Actionstep?
paragraphs: Actionstep is a cloud-based legal practice management platform that combines matter management, workflow automation, billing, trust accounting, and CRM tools, with AI features for time capture and client intake.
The company operates 5 global offices, including its Denver, CO location, and offers 50+ integrations such as Microsoft Office 365, Xero, Zapier, and DocuSign.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Actionstep is for
paragraphs: Actionstep is built for midsize law firms across practice areas including bankruptcy, business, criminal defense, employment, estate planning, family, immigration, intellectual property, litigation, personal injury, real estate, and tax law.

enrichment.short_description: Practice management software built for personal injury law firms, with an AI writing assistant.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: 8am CASEpeer is personal injury practice management software starting at $79/user/month with an AI writing assistant.

features.title: Case & contact management
features.description: Centralizes case details, documents, deadlines, and liens.

features.title: Medical treatment tracking
features.description: Tracks treatment, medical records requests, and provider management.

features.title: Settlement negotiations
features.description: Tools to monitor and manage settlement progress.

features.title: 8am IQ writing assistant
features.description: AI tool for polishing case notes, correcting grammar, adjusting tone, and translating between English, Spanish, and Arabic (Pro tier and up).

features.title: Client intake management
features.description: Manages incoming leads and intake investigator workflows (Pro and Advanced tiers).

features.title: Reporting
features.description: 50+ reports covering high-value cases, medical requests, and firm productivity.

features.title: API access
features.description: Available on the Advanced plan for custom integrations.

pros: Purpose-built for personal injury law firms with pre-configured workflows
4.8/5 on Capterra and 4.7/5 on G2 across 400+ verified reviews
8am IQ AI writing assistant supports English, Spanish, and Arabic translation
No long-term contract or setup fee; upgrade, downgrade, or cancel anytime
cons: No free trial mentioned; only a free demo is offered
Pricing is per active user and scales quickly for larger teams
API access and multi-office reporting are limited to the top Advanced tier

faqs.question: How much does CASEpeer cost?
faqs.answer: CASEpeer starts at $79/user/month for the Basic plan, with Pro at $119/user/month and Advanced at $149/user/month, billed monthly.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: CASEpeer does not mention a free trial; a free demo is available instead.

faqs.question: What is 8am IQ?
faqs.answer: 8am IQ is CASEpeer's AI writing assistant, available from the Pro plan up, that polishes case notes and can translate between English, Spanish, and Arabic.

faqs.question: What practice areas does CASEpeer support?
faqs.answer: CASEpeer is built specifically for personal injury law firms.

use_cases.title: Intake-to-settlement case tracking
use_cases.description: Personal injury firms use CASEpeer to manage cases from intake through settlement without custom configuration.

use_cases.title: Multi-office reporting
use_cases.description: Larger personal injury firms use the Advanced plan's multi-office reporting suite and API access for custom integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CASEpeer?
paragraphs: CASEpeer (8am CASEpeer) is a cloud-based practice management platform built specifically for personal injury law firms, covering case management from intake through settlement without requiring customization.

It offers three published pricing tiers, from $79 to $149 per user per month, and includes 8am IQ, an AI writing assistant that polishes case notes and supports English, Spanish, and Arabic translation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who CASEpeer is for
paragraphs: CASEpeer is aimed at personal injury law firms of various sizes, particularly those without dedicated IT resources who want pre-configured workflows and a fast rollout.
